message:
refactor(btree): extract inline cell pointer and child page readers for allocation-free traversal (bd-wwqen.1)

Extract two helper methods from BtCursor to support allocation-free B-tree
traversal needed by count_all_rows_iterative:

1. read_child_at_offset(): Reads a 4-byte big-endian child page number
   directly from raw page bytes at a given cell offset. Factored out of
   the existing read_child_page_no() to decouple the child pointer read
   from the cell_pointers Vec lookup.

2. read_cell_pointer_inline(): Reads a single cell pointer entry at a given
   index directly from the page header's cell pointer array, computing the
   offset arithmetically (header_offset + header_size + cell_idx * 2).
   This avoids allocating the full cell_pointers Vec when only sequential
   access is needed during iterative row counting.

Both methods maintain the same corruption detection (bounds checks with
DatabaseCorrupt errors) as the original Vec-based path. This enables
count_all_rows_iterative to scan interior pages without any heap allocation
per page, reducing GC pressure on large table scans.

Also updates beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
